title: carex depressa subsp. transsilvanica
intro: Carex depressa subsp. transsilvanica, also known as the Transylvanian sedge, is a subspecies of sedge plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Carex depressa subsp. transsilvanica include its ability to soothe and calm the respiratory system, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Carex depressa subsp. transsilvanica is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, where the dried plant material is steeped in hot water to release its medicinal properties.
